description:
A research assistant has left you a directory of raw experiment logs and
    asked for help preparing a cleaned-up “critical events” data set.

    Files that are already present:
      /home/user/research/logs/raw/run1.log
      /home/user/research/logs/raw/run2.log
      /home/user/research/logs/raw/run3.log

    Log-line format (every line in every file follows this pattern exactly):
        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm [service_name] LEVEL: message text …

        • LEVEL is one of: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, FATAL
        • There is precisely one space between each token shown above.
        • Timestamp milliseconds are always three digits.

    Requested result
    ----------------
    1.  Create the directory /home/user/research/logs/combined
        and inside it create a file named full.log that contains
        ALL lines from every *.log file in /home/user/research/logs/raw/,
        sorted in ascending timestamp order.  Do NOT lose or alter
        whitespace; the lines in full.log must be byte-for-byte identical
        to the original lines.

    2.  Create the directory /home/user/research/logs/filtered
        and inside it create a file named critical.log that contains
        ONLY the lines whose LEVEL is WARNING, ERROR or FATAL,
        in the same order they appear in full.log.

    3.  Still inside /home/user/research/logs/filtered create
        summary.csv containing a compact per-level count of the lines that
        were written to critical.log.  The CSV must have exact, literal
        contents (including capitalisation and line breaks) in this form:

            level,count
            WARNING,<number_of_warning_lines>
            ERROR,<number_of_error_lines>
            FATAL,<number_of_fatal_lines>

        • Keep the order WARNING → ERROR → FATAL exactly as shown.
        • Do not include any other columns or rows.
        • <number_of_*_lines> must be the integer counts, no extra spaces.

    Verification notes
    ------------------
    • The automated grader will compare files using the UNIX diff utility,
      so every byte (including final newline characters) must match.
    • The grader will check that:
        – /home/user/research/logs/combined/full.log exists and has the
          correct 15 lines in chronological order.  
        – /home/user/research/logs/filtered/critical.log exists and has
          the correct 6 lines (WARNING, ERROR and FATAL only).  
        – /home/user/research/logs/filtered/summary.csv exists and
          matches the exact three-row format shown above with the correct
          counts 3, 2 and 1 respectively.
    • You do NOT need sudo and must work only within /home/user.
    • Show any terminal output you deem useful, but make sure the three
      files described above end up at the exact paths (they will be
      read directly by the tests).
truth:

        2023-05-01 09:00:02.789 [data_collector] WARNING: Sensor 1 value out of range

        2023-05-01 09:00:04.345 [data_collector] ERROR: Failed to write to database

        2023-05-01 09:00:01.456 [data_collector] DEBUG: Reading sensor 1
        2023-05-01 09:00:02.789 [data_collector] WARNING: Sensor 1 value out of range

        2023-05-01 10:10:05.555 [data_processor] INFO: Completed batch job
        2023-05-01 10:10:06.666 [data_processor] DEBUG: Post-processing

        2023-05-01 11:20:00.000 [report_generator] DEBUG: loading templates
        2023-05-01 11:20:01.000 [report_generator] INFO: generating report

        2023-05-01 11:20:03.000 [report_generator] INFO: report generated
